channel.title: Home on QA & University Performance Department
channel.description: Recent content in Home on QA & University Performance Department

item.title: The Quality Assurance and University Performance.
item.description:
The Quality Assurance and University Performance Department at Al-Qadisiyah University held a workshop on the requirements of the first standard for national institutional accreditation: institutional strategy and development mechanisms.

Under the patronage of the President of Al-Qadisiyah University, Professor Dr. Rahi Kalaf Mahdi Al-Yasiri, and the supervision of the Director of the Quality Assurance and University Performance Department, Professor Dr. Abbas Sabbar Dakhel, the department, in coordination with the Center for Developing University Teaching Methods and Training, held a workshop entitled:

item.title: Accessibility
item.description:
Accessibility Statement

Al-Qadisiyah University is committed to making its website accessible to all users.

Our Commitments

  • Design compliant with WCAG 2.1 standards
  • Screen reader support
  • Appropriate color contrast
  • Alternative text for images
  • Keyboard navigation

Assistance

If you encounter any accessibility difficulties:

  • Email: accessibility@qu.edu.iq
  • Phone: +964 780 000 0000

Report an Issue

We welcome your feedback to improve accessibility.

item.title: Arab Rankings
item.description:
Tracked Arab Rankings

QS Arab Region University Rankings

A regional edition of the QS ranking, focused on universities in Arab countries; criteria include academic reputation, employer reputation, faculty-to-student ratio, research, and internationalization.

4ICU Arab Region

A web-based ranking produced by 4ICU that measures the web presence and online impact of Arab universities.

Association of Arab Universities Ranking

A ranking issued by the Association of Arab Universities measuring the academic, research and social performance of member institutions.

item.title: Contact Us
item.description:
Contact Information

Address

Quality Assurance and University Performance Department
Al-Qadisiyah University — Presidency
Al-Diwaniyah, Al-Qadisiyah Province, Iraq

Email

  • Departmental email: qu.assurance@qu.edu.iq

Working Hours

  • Sunday - Thursday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM
  • Friday & Saturday: Closed

Contact Form

To contact the department, please fill out the form below and we will respond as soon as possible.

The contact form is not currently enabled. Please email the department directly at: qu.assurance@qu.edu.iq

Location

item.title: Data Protection
item.description:
Data Protection Policy

Al-Qadisiyah University is committed to protecting personal data of all users according to international standards.

Data Protection Principles

  1. Transparency - Informing users how their data is used
  2. Minimization - Collecting only necessary data
  3. Accuracy - Maintaining data accuracy and updates
  4. Security - Protecting data from unauthorized access

Data Subject Rights

  • Right to access data
  • Right to rectification
  • Right to erasure
  • Right to restrict processing
  • Right to data portability

Contact

  • Data Protection Officer: dpo@qu.edu.iq

item.title: Department Structure
item.description:
About the Department

The Quality Assurance and University Performance Department at Al-Qadisiyah University was established in December 2017 as the unit responsible for applying the quality and accreditation system — institutional, programmatic and laboratory — across all colleges, centers and administrative units of the university.

The department coordinates with the university presidency, all scientific and administrative departments, and relevant external bodies (the Supervision and Scientific Evaluation Authority, the Quality Assurance and Academic Accreditation Directorate at the Ministry of Higher Education, and international accreditation bodies).

item.title: Department Tasks
item.description:
Principal Tasks

Strategy and Planning

  • Preparing and periodically updating the university’s strategic plan for quality assurance
  • Preparing annual improvement plans for colleges and centers
  • Proposing policies and procedures required to operate the quality system

Institutional and Programmatic Accreditation

  • Following up institutional accreditation procedures in line with the Ministry of Higher Education’s standards
  • Supporting colleges and departments in obtaining programmatic accreditation
  • Liaising with international accreditation bodies and submitting required files
  • Tracking ISO certifications across the university

Performance Evaluation

  • Preparing annual institutional performance-evaluation forms
  • Distributing forms to colleges and centers and monitoring their application
  • Collecting and analyzing data and issuing reports
  • Publishing results and submitting recommendations to relevant authorities

Rankings

  • Tracking the university’s position in international and Arab rankings
  • Collecting and formatting data per the requirements of each ranking
  • Liaising with ranking bodies (Times Higher Education, QS, Webometrics, Scimago, IRU)
  • Producing annual reports on ranking outcomes

Laboratory Accreditation

  • Inventorying laboratories across all colleges
  • Documenting laboratory equipment and capabilities
  • Pursuing laboratory accreditation in line with ISO/IEC 17025
  • Disseminating laboratory safety standards and monitoring compliance

Promoting a Quality Culture

  • Organizing training workshops on quality assurance and accreditation
  • Preparing and circulating guidance manuals and standards
  • Participating in conferences and seminars on quality
  • Issuing awareness publications and bulletins

Surveys and Feedback

  • Designing and implementing student-feedback surveys on teaching staff
  • Designing student-satisfaction surveys on university services
  • Designing employer-satisfaction surveys on graduates
  • Analyzing results and submitting recommendations

External Coordination

  • Coordinating with the Quality Assurance and Academic Accreditation Directorate at the Ministry
  • Coordinating with the Supervision and Scientific Evaluation Authority
  • Coordinating with international bodies concerned with quality and accreditation
  • Maintaining memberships and subscriptions

item.title: Documents & Records
item.description:
Documents and Records

The Quality Assurance Department provides the reference documentation that colleges and laboratories need to complete laboratory accreditation procedures in line with the adopted international standards.

Document Types

Standards Documents

  • ISO/IEC 17025:2017
  • Implementation guidance manuals
  • Detailed accreditation requirements

Procedural Documents

  • Laboratory quality manual
  • Laboratory operating procedures
  • Equipment calibration procedures
  • Result-verification procedures

Records

  • Training records
  • Maintenance records
  • Test records
  • Customer-complaint records
  • Non-conformity records

Forms

  • Test-request forms
  • Test-report forms
  • Laboratory-evaluation forms
  • Management-review forms

References

  • ISO/IEC 17025:2017 — General requirements for the competence of testing and calibration laboratories
  • ILAC G19 — Modules in a Forensic Science Process
  • ISO 15189:2022 — Medical laboratories
  • Iraqi Ministry of Higher Education and Scientific Research guidance

To obtain documents: contact the Accreditation Division in the department.

item.title: Guidance Manual
item.description:
Institutional Performance-Evaluation Guidance Manual

The guidance manual serves as a reference document for colleges and centers when completing the institutional performance-evaluation forms. It aims to standardize concepts and ensure the quality of data submitted.

Manual Contents

Chapter One: Introduction

  • Overview of the institutional performance-evaluation system
  • General objectives
  • Bodies responsible for implementation

Chapter Two: Concepts and Terminology

  • Definitions of the principal indicators
  • Units of measurement
  • Methodology for calculating ratios

Chapter Three: Completion Instructions

  • Steps for completing the form
  • Required evidence and documentation
  • Deadlines

Chapter Four: Verification and Audit

  • Internal review mechanism
  • External audit
  • Feedback loops

Annexes

  • Form templates
  • Checklists
  • Worked examples
  • Indicator tables

To obtain a copy of the manual: contact the department at: qu.assurance@qu.edu.iq

item.title: Institutional Accreditation
item.description:
Institutional Accreditation

Institutional accreditation is a comprehensive evaluation conducted at the level of the university as a whole to verify that the established standards for ensuring the quality of educational, research and administrative outputs are met. Accreditation is granted by the Ministry of Higher Education and Scientific Research through the Quality Assurance and Academic Accreditation Directorate.

Institutional Accreditation Standards

The institutional accreditation framework is built on nine principal standards:

  1. Mission, Vision and Objectives: clarity and alignment with societal needs
  2. Administration and Governance: organizational structures and decision-making responsibilities
  3. Academic Programs: programme design and alignment with national and international standards
  4. Faculty and Staff: qualifications, competencies and professional-development opportunities
  5. Students and Student Services: admission, registration, advising, activities
  6. Research and Innovation: research environment, outputs, impact
  7. Learning Resources and Infrastructure: libraries, laboratories, technologies
  8. Community Service and Partnerships: cooperation with institutions and the private sector
  9. Quality Assurance: policies, procedures and improvement mechanisms

Role of the Department

The Quality Assurance Department prepares the annual institutional accreditation file, gathers evidence and documentation from all colleges and centers, and submits the report to the relevant Ministry authorities.

item.title: Institutional Evaluation Forms 2023-2024
item.description:
Forms for the 2023-2024 Academic Year

Archive of the institutional performance-evaluation forms applied at Al-Qadisiyah University during the 2023-2024 academic year.

Evaluation Pillars

Academic Pillar

  • Academic programmes and their updating
  • Learning outcomes and their assessment
  • Teaching and assessment methods
  • Faculty research output

Administrative Pillar

  • Organizational structure
  • Human-resources management
  • Financial resources
  • Administrative organization

Student Pillar

  • Student services
  • Extracurricular activities
  • Academic advising
  • Programme graduates

Infrastructure Pillar

  • Lecture halls
  • Laboratories
  • Libraries
  • Technology and e-systems

Note: The annual report covering the 2023-2024 evaluation outcomes has been issued and is available to authorized university bodies.

item.title: Institutional Evaluation Forms 2024-2025
item.description:
Forms for the 2024-2025 Academic Year

The 2024-2025 institutional performance-evaluation framework comprises an integrated set of forms directed at colleges, centers, and administrative departments.

Form Types

College Performance Form

Covering:

  • Academic programmes and learning outcomes
  • Faculty and staff
  • Students and student services
  • Research and creative activity
  • Infrastructure and laboratories
  • Community service and partnerships
  • Internal quality assurance

Research-Centre Performance Form

Covering:

  • Research output
  • Laboratories and equipment
  • External collaboration
  • Patents and innovation

Administrative-Department Performance Form

Covering:

item.title: International Rankings
item.description:
Monitored International Rankings

Times Higher Education World University Ranking

The ranking evaluates the performance of research universities based on their core missions and includes the following criteria:

  • Teaching and the learning environment: Includes academic reputation and the student-to-staff ratio.
  • Research environment: Measures research volume, research income, and research reputation.
  • Research quality: Focuses on citation impact, research strength, excellence, and global influence.
  • International outlook: Measures the proportion of international students, staff, and cross-border collaborative research.
  • Industry and innovation: Measures the amount of income the university generates from the industry sector and patents.

University Impact Ranking

item.title: Iraqi Ranking of Universities (IRU)
item.description:
Iraqi Ranking of Universities (IRU)

The Iraqi Ranking of Universities (IRU) is a national ranking produced by the Ministry of Higher Education and Scientific Research in cooperation with the Research and Development Directorate. It evaluates Iraqi universities against a unified set of indicators covering teaching quality, research, and community service.

  • 2025 Ranking

Ranking Pillars

  • Teaching and Learning: programme quality, faculty-to-student ratio, educational infrastructure
  • Scientific Research: publications in international databases, citations, patents
  • Community Service: community engagement, consultations, training
  • Internationalization: cooperation agreements, international students, academic exchange
  • Resources and Administration: administrative efficiency, human and financial resources
  • Governance and Transparency: policies, procedures, reporting

Note: The ranking is released periodically. The Performance Evaluation Division in the department collects and submits the required data to the national ranking committee.

item.title: ISO International Certifications
item.description:
ISO Certifications

International Organization for Standardization (ISO) certifications are among the most widely recognized quality standards worldwide. The Quality Assurance Department tracks the attainment and periodic renewal of ISO certifications relevant to the university’s operations.

Relevant Certifications

ISO 9001 — Quality Management Systems

An international standard that defines the requirements for a quality management system in an organization seeking to deliver products or services that meet customer and regulatory requirements.

item.title: Laboratory Inventory
item.description:
Laboratory Inventory

The Quality Assurance Department maintains an up-to-date database of every laboratory across the colleges and centers of Al-Qadisiyah University, covering:

Inventory Data

Basic Information

  • Laboratory name
  • Owning college or department
  • Area and location
  • Laboratory supervisor
  • Disciplines served

Equipment

  • List of major instruments
  • Year of acquisition
  • Operational status
  • Maintenance schedule

Capabilities

  • Approved tests and analyses
  • Services provided to the university
  • Services provided to external parties
  • Research outputs

Inventory Objectives

  • Maintain a comprehensive database to inform management decisions
  • Identify needs and gaps
  • Plan expansions and upgrades
  • Facilitate inter-laboratory cooperation
  • Prepare accreditation files

Updating the Inventory

Inventory data is updated annually via dedicated forms circulated to the colleges; submissions are reviewed in the department before being formally adopted.

item.title: Laboratory Landscape
item.description:
Laboratory Landscape at Al-Qadisiyah University

Al-Qadisiyah University hosts a wide network of scientific, teaching and research laboratories distributed across its colleges; they are a cornerstone of both the academic and the research mission.

Laboratories by College

College of Medicine

  • Anatomy laboratories
  • Physiology laboratories
  • Biochemistry laboratories
  • Microbiology laboratories
  • Histology and pathology laboratories

College of Pharmacy

  • Pharmaceutical-chemistry laboratories
  • Pharmaceutics laboratories
  • Pharmacognosy laboratories
  • Toxicology laboratories

College of Engineering

  • Mechanical laboratories
  • Electrical and electronics laboratories
  • Fluid and thermal laboratories
  • Materials and structures laboratories

College of Science

  • Chemistry laboratories
  • Physics laboratories
  • Biology laboratories
  • Earth-sciences laboratories

College of Agriculture

  • Soil and water laboratories
  • Plant-production laboratories
  • Animal-production laboratories
  • Food-technology laboratories

College of Veterinary Medicine

  • Parasitology laboratories
  • Animal-pathology laboratories
  • Animal-production laboratories

College of Computer Science and IT

  • Programming laboratories
  • Networks laboratories
  • Artificial-intelligence laboratories

Challenges and Opportunities

  • Some equipment requires modernization
  • Expanding accreditation to additional laboratories
  • Strengthening inter-laboratory cooperation
  • Building the capacity of technical staff

item.title: Laboratory Safety
item.description:
Laboratory Safety

Laboratory safety is a top priority at Al-Qadisiyah University; the Quality Assurance Department promotes a culture of safety across every laboratory and monitors its implementation.

Principal Standards

Personal Safety

  • Wearing personal protective equipment (PPE) — lab coat, safety glasses, gloves
  • Knowing the location of safety equipment (fire extinguisher, safety shower, eyewash)
  • No food or drink inside the laboratory
  • Tying back long hair and wearing closed-toe shoes

Handling Chemicals

  • Reading safety data sheets (SDS) before working
  • Proper storage of chemicals
  • Using a fume hood when working with volatile substances
  • Segregating incompatible materials

Electrical Safety

  • Inspecting equipment before use
  • Not using damaged equipment
  • Disconnecting from the power source after use
  • Avoiding overloading outlets

Biosafety

  • Operating at the biosafety level appropriate to the laboratory
  • Mandatory vaccinations for relevant staff
  • Safe disposal of biohazardous waste
  • Sterilizing surfaces and tools

Waste Management

  • Segregating waste by type (chemical, biological, sharps, infectious)
  • Using dedicated containers
  • Coordinating with safe-disposal contractors
  • Documenting disposal operations

In Emergencies

Fire

  • Switch off electrical equipment
  • Use the appropriate fire extinguisher
  • Evacuate toward the nearest emergency exit
  • Call emergency services

Chemical Spills

  • Evacuate the area
  • Notify the laboratory supervisor
  • Use protective equipment and follow clean-up procedures
  • Document the incident

Injuries

  • Use the first-aid kit
  • Transfer the injured person to medical emergency services if needed
  • Report immediately and document

Training

The department offers periodic training workshops for laboratory staff on safety procedures, in cooperation with the colleges of Medicine, Pharmacy and Engineering.

item.title: Memberships & Subscriptions
item.description:
Memberships and Subscriptions

Al-Qadisiyah University is keen to join international bodies and unions concerned with quality and academic accreditation, given the value of exchanging expertise and keeping up with developments in the field.

Principal Memberships

Association of Arab Universities (AArU)

Active membership in a union of Arab universities that coordinates academic and research cooperation across Arab states.

International Network for Quality Assurance Agencies in Higher Education (INQAAHE)

An international network of more than 300 quality-assurance agencies from around the world.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Introduction

Al-Qadisiyah University is committed to protecting the privacy of its website visitors and users. This policy explains how we collect, use, and protect personal information.

Information We Collect

Information You Provide

  • Name and contact details when filling out contact forms
  • Email address when subscribing to newsletters
  • Academic information when applying for admission

Automatically Collected Information

  • IP address and browser type
  • Pages visited on the website
  • Date and time of visit
  • Cookies

How We Use Information

We use collected information for the following purposes:

item.title: Programmatic Accreditation
item.description:
Programmatic Accreditation

Programmatic accreditation is a qualitative evaluation conducted at the level of a single academic programme to verify the quality of its design, implementation and learning outcomes against recognized local or international standards.

Types of Programmatic Accreditation

National Accreditation

Granted by the Quality Assurance and Academic Accreditation Directorate at the Iraqi Ministry of Higher Education in accordance with the national academic programme standards.

International Accreditation

  • ABET: for engineering and computer-science programmes
  • AACSB: for business school programmes
  • ASIIN: for science, engineering, informatics and mathematics programmes
  • ACPE / WFME: for pharmacy and medical programmes
  • EUR-ACE: for engineering programmes in the European area

Accreditation Stages

  1. Self-Assessment: the programme prepares a detailed self-study report against the standards
  2. External Visit: a team of independent experts visits and evaluates the programme
  3. Final Report: the team submits its findings together with recommendations
  4. Decision: the accrediting body issues its decision (full accreditation, conditional, or denial)
  5. Follow-up: periodic monitoring to verify continued compliance

Role of the Department

The Quality Assurance Department supports colleges and academic departments in preparing programmatic accreditation files, provides the necessary guidance and training, and coordinates with local and international accrediting bodies.

item.title: Security Policy
item.description:
Introduction

Al-Qadisiyah University is committed to providing a secure digital environment for all users. This policy outlines the security procedures and measures implemented to protect the website and data.

SSL Certificate and Encryption

  • Our website uses a valid SSL certificate to encrypt all communications
  • All transmitted data is protected with HTTPS encryption
  • HSTS policy is implemented to ensure secure connections

Attack Protection

DDoS Protection

  • Advanced protection system against denial-of-service attacks
  • Continuous monitoring of abnormal traffic

SQL Injection Protection

  • Inspection and filtering of all inputs
  • Use of parameterized queries

XSS Protection

  • Sanitization of user-submitted content
  • Content Security Policies (CSP)

Firewall

  • Web Application Firewall (WAF)
  • Blocklists for suspicious addresses
  • Monitoring and logging of intrusion attempts

Security Testing

We conduct regular security tests including:

item.title: Sitemap
item.description:
Sitemap

Main Sections

  • Home
  • About the Department
  • Classifications
  • Accreditations & Memberships
  • Performance Evaluation
  • Laboratory Accreditation
  • Key Statistics
  • Guides & Standards
  • Work Team
  • News
  • Contact

XML Sitemap

item.title: Surveys
item.description:
Surveys

The department designs and administers a range of surveys directed at the various beneficiaries of university services, in order to measure satisfaction and gather feedback for performance improvement.

Principal Surveys

Faculty Evaluation Survey (Students)

Administered at the end of each semester to measure student satisfaction with teaching-staff performance; results feed into teaching-performance evaluation and professional-development plans.

Programme Satisfaction Survey (Students)

Measures students’ satisfaction with academic programmes in terms of content, teaching and assessment methods, and available resources.

item.title: Vision & Mission
item.description:
Vision

To establish a quality culture as deeply rooted expertise among the scientific and administrative staff of Al-Qadisiyah University, and to apply local, regional and international standards in education and scientific research in alignment with sustainable development goals.

Mission

To apply quality-assurance standards in order to provide a sustainable educational environment, and to pursue continuous improvement through institutional and programmatic accreditation while tracking every relevant development in the field of quality and accreditation at the local and international levels.
